events.title: Open House New York: The Blockhouse
events.description: Event page for Open House New York at Chess & Checkers House in Central Park, NYC

schema_org.description: Event page for Open House New York at Chess & Checkers House in Central Park, NYC
schema_org.name: Open House New York: The Blockhouse

detail_page_data.location_detail: Blockhouse, Central Park's North Woods (prominent rock outcrop)
detail_page_data.description_detail: Visitors can see inside Central Park's oldest surviving structure, constructed as part of a fortification system during the War of 1812. Central Park Conservancy expert historians share details about the location's historical significance.
detail_page_data.organizer: Open House New York in partnership with Central Park Conservancy

events.title: Juneteenth in Seneca Village (2025)
events.summary: This event is part of Seneca Village: Toward a Permanent Commemoration, an ongoing program series funded by the Mellon Foundation.
events.description: This event is part of Seneca Village: Toward a Permanent Commemoration, an ongoing program series funded by the Mellon Foundation.

schema_org.description: This event is part of Seneca Village: Toward a Permanent Commemoration, an ongoing program series funded by the Mellon Foundation.
schema_org.name: Juneteenth in Seneca Village (2025)

detail_page_data.description_detail: Celebration featuring an intergenerational community of musicians and poets in the historic Seneca Village area. Live musical performances and poetry readings inspired by former residents' experiences.
performers: The Rakiem Walker Project (Rakiem Walker and GLRYA)
Kai Diata Giovanni (2024-2025 NYC Youth Poet Laureate)
Kayden Hern (2023 New York Gubernatorial Inauguration Poet Laureate)
detail_page_data.program_series: Seneca Village: Toward a Permanent Commemoration (Mellon Foundation)

events.title: Tour: Water in Focus
events.summary: Join us for a spotlight on how water shapes the visitor experience in the Park’s north end.
events.description: Join us for a spotlight on how water shapes the visitor experience in the Park’s north end.

detail_page_data.description_detail: Tour exploring how water shapes the visitor experience in the Park's north end. Covers natural waterways, aqueduct history, decorative fountains, winter ice skating traditions, and summer swimming. No upcoming dates currently scheduled.

events.title: Founders’ Day: 200th Anniversary of Seneca Village
events.summary: Join the Central Park Conservancy for a music-filled service at AME Zion Church in Harlem to honor the 200th anniversary of the first land purchase in Seneca Village.
events.description: Join the Central Park Conservancy for a music-filled service at AME Zion Church in Harlem to honor the 200th anniversary of the first land purchase in Seneca Village.

schema_org.description: Join the Central Park Conservancy for a music-filled service at AME Zion Church in Harlem to honor the 200th anniversary of the first land purchase in Seneca Village.
schema_org.name: Founders’ Day: 200th Anniversary of Seneca Village

detail_page_data.time_detail: Church Service: 11:00 AM; Refreshments/Open House: 12:30 PM - 1:00 PM; Discussion: 1:00 PM
detail_page_data.location_detail: Mother AME Zion Church, 140 West 137th Street, New York, NY 10030 (Harlem)
detail_page_data.description_detail: Music-filled service at the historic AME Zion Church in Harlem - the first Black church in New York City - to honor the 200th anniversary of when congregant Andrew Williams and the trustees of the AME Zion Church were among the first to purchase land in what became Seneca Village (between West 82nd and 89th Streets).
program_components: Presentation by Institute for the Exploration of Seneca Village History and Envisioning Seneca Village team
detail_page_data.program_series: Seneca Village: Toward a Permanent Commemoration (Mellon Foundation)

events.title: Evening at the Water
events.summary: Thank you for joining the Greensward Circle, the Central Park Conservancy’s network of young professionals, at their summer benefit: Evening at the Water.
events.description: We hope you enjoyed the evening and can join us again in 2025! Your support enables the Conservancy to preserve and celebrate Central Park as a sanctuary from the pace and pressures of city life, enhancing the enjoyment and wellbeing of all.  Join today for exclusive access to upcoming Greensward Circle events such as Yoga in the Park, volunteer days, happy hours, and more!   Questions? Call 212.776.1061 or email greenswardcircle@centralparknyc.org .   Greensward Circle Co-Chairs  Alice Cash & Rebecca Malizia   Host Committee: Brittany Beyer Harwin Blythe Bonan Alice Cash Caroline Colella Elaine Dealy Whitney Dosik Naomi Drucker Lindsay Hoeppner Courtney Kelly Madison Ladymon Kaitlyn Lynch Rebecca Malizia Jeffrey McHugh Ashely Post Alayna Putterman Brian Reinke Raquel Ringgold Lauren Kiana Souchère Katie Turner Ruth Zapata Megan Zuckerman

events.title: Chess Lecture & Simul Series with Beginners Workshops
events.summary: Learn play techniques from world-renowned chess experts and put your skills to the test! After the lecture and simul, beginners can learn the basics of the game.
events.description: Learn play techniques from world-renowned chess experts and put your skills to the test! After the lecture and simul, beginners can learn the basics of…

detail_page_data.description_detail: Learn play techniques from world-renowned chess experts and put your skills to the test. Following the lecture and simultaneous exhibition, beginners receive instruction in fundamental gameplay.
detail_page_data.status: This year's program has concluded; Chess & Checkers House remains open year-round

events.title: Seneca Village: Toward a Permanent Commemoration (2025)
events.summary: This year, the 200th anniversary of the inception of Seneca Village, the Conservancy launched a multiyear program series aimed at deepening understanding of this thriving pre-park community.
events.description: This year, the 200th anniversary of the inception of Seneca Village, the Conservancy launched a multiyear program series aimed at deepening understanding of this thriving pre-park community.

schema_org.description: This year, the 200th anniversary of the inception of Seneca Village, the Conservancy launched a multiyear program series aimed at deepening understanding of this thriving pre-park community.
schema_org.name: Seneca Village: Toward a Permanent Commemoration (2025)

detail_page_data.location_detail: Schomburg Center for Research in Black Culture, Harlem
detail_page_data.description_detail: Inaugural launch event for a multiyear program series supported by the Mellon Foundation. Aims to deepen understanding of this thriving pre-park community and develop a community-informed framework for the permanent commemoration of Seneca Village in Central Park.
highlights: Presentations by City Councilmember Dr. Yusef Salaam and Assemblymember Jordan J.G. Wright
Reception in the Langston Hughes Lobby
detail_page_data.note: 2025 marks the 200th anniversary of Seneca Village's inception

events.title: Central Park Farm Stand
events.summary: The Central Park Farm Stand offers fresh, seasonal produce grown with care by BIPOC-owned farms.
events.description: The Central Park Farm Stand offers fresh, seasonal produce grown with care by BIPOC-owned farms.

schema_org.description: The Central Park Farm Stand offers fresh, seasonal produce grown with care by BIPOC-owned farms.
schema_org.name: Central Park Farm Stand

detail_page_data.location_detail: 110th Street & Malcolm X Boulevard, Central Park
detail_page_data.description_detail: Partnership between the Central Park Conservancy and Corbin Hill Food Project offering fresh, seasonal produce from BIPOC-owned farms in the Hudson Valley. Promotes food access, sustainable agriculture, and supports Black and Brown farmers.
detail_page_data.status: Currently closed for the season; operates summer and fall months

events.title: 29th Annual Central Park Holiday Lighting
events.summary: Celebrate the season with the 29th Annual Holiday Lighting in Central Park! Meet Santa and friends, sing carols on the plaza, and warm up with hot cocoa while enjoying a variety of seasonal activities.
events.description: Celebrate the season with the 29th Annual Holiday Lighting in Central Park! Meet Santa and friends, sing carols on the plaza, and warm up with hot cocoa while enjoying a variety of seasonal activities.

schema_org.description: Celebrate the season with the 29th Annual Holiday Lighting in Central Park! Meet Santa and friends, sing carols on the plaza, and warm up with hot cocoa while enjoying a variety of seasonal activities.
schema_org.name: 29th Annual Central Park Holiday Lighting

detail_page_data.location_detail: Charles A. Dana Discovery Center, Central Park
detail_page_data.description_detail: Annual holiday celebration featuring meeting Santa and seasonal characters, group carol singing on the plaza, warm beverages including hot cocoa, and various seasonal activities. Free coloring pages, carol songbooks, and holiday recipes available for download.
detail_page_data.contact: 212-310-6600 or subscribe to Programs and Events mailing list for 2026 details

events.title: Before, there was Seneca Village! (2025)
events.summary: The Central Park Conservancy invited community members to join us in the historic Seneca Village landscape in Central Park for a performance that transported them back to Seneca Village in 1845.
events.description: The Central Park Conservancy invited community members to join us in the historic Seneca Village landscape in Central Park for a performance that transported them back to Seneca Village in 1845.

schema_org.description: The Central Park Conservancy invited community members to join us in the historic Seneca Village landscape in Central Park for a performance that transported them back to Seneca Village in 1845.
schema_org.name: Before, there was Seneca Village! (2025)

detail_page_data.description_detail: Commemorative performance bringing 1845 Seneca Village to life through interpretive music, dance, and spoken word. Explored how Andrew Williams and others' 1825 land purchases developed into a thriving community where Black New Yorkers built their own homes, churches, and a school.
detail_page_data.program_series: Seneca Village: Toward a Permanent Commemoration (Mellon Foundation)

events.title: Central Park Conservancy's Annual Gala
events.summary: This year’s event, the Central Park Supper Club, will be an unforgettable evening of cocktails, dinner, dancing and entertainment.
events.description: Thank you to the guests and patrons who supported the Central Park Conservancy's 2025 Annual Gala. We hope you enjoyed the evening and can join us again in 2026! For more information, please contact the Special Events department at 212.446.2242 or specialevents@centralparknyc.org .

schema_org.description: Thank you to the guests and patrons who supported the Central Park Conservancy's 2025 Annual Gala. We hope you enjoyed the evening and can join us again in 2026! schema_org.name: Central Park Conservancy's Annual Gala

detail_page_data.description_detail: Annual fundraising gala. The 2025 event theme was 'Central Park Supper Club' featuring cocktails, dinner, dancing and entertainment.

events.title: Before, there was Seneca Village!
events.summary: Watch as Seneca Village is brought to life through an interpretive performance.
events.description: Watch as Seneca Village is brought to life through an interpretive performance.

schema_org.description: Watch as Seneca Village is brought to life through an interpretive performance.
schema_org.name: Before, there was Seneca Village!

detail_page_data.location_detail: Seneca Village Landscape, Central Park, between West 85th Street and West Drive. Enter at West 85th Street.
detail_page_data.description_detail: Interpretive performance bringing Seneca Village to life as it existed in 1845. Features music, dance, and spoken word exploring how Andrew Williams's 1825 land purchase developed into a thriving Black community with homes, churches, and a school.
detail_page_data.program_series: Seneca Village: Toward a Permanent Commemoration (Mellon Foundation)

events.title: Wildlife Park Safari
events.summary: Join us for a tour highlighting the interconnected, viable ecosystems of the Park's North End.
events.description: Join us for a tour highlighting the interconnected, viable ecosystems of the Park's North End.

detail_page_data.description_detail: Tour highlighting the thriving, interconnected ecosystems of the Park's north end. Participants learn five basic principles for beginner birders and explore wildlife and self-sustaining ecosystems while discovering conservation efforts.
